title: opt_expr prevents driver/constant conflict warning
body:
```verilog
module top(input [3:0] a, output [3:0] y);
	wire [3:0] x = a + 1;
	wire [3:0] tmp = 0;
	assign tmp = x;
	assign y = tmp | x;
endmodule
```

Running `yosys -qp "opt_clean" driver_constant.v` gives
```
Warning: Driver-driver conflict for $add$driver_constant.v:2$1_Y [3] between cell $add$driver_constant.v:2$1.Y and constant 1'0 in top: Resolved using constant.
Warning: Driver-driver conflict for $add$driver_constant.v:2$1_Y [2] between cell $add$driver_constant.v:2$1.Y and constant 1'0 in top: Resolved using constant.
Warning: Driver-driver conflict for $add$driver_constant.v:2$1_Y [1] between cell $add$driver_constant.v:2$1.Y and constant 1'0 in top: Resolved using constant.
Warning: Driver-driver conflict for $add$driver_constant.v:2$1_Y [0] between cell $add$driver_constant.v:2$1.Y and constant 1'0 in top: Resolved using constant.
```

Running `yosys -qp "opt_expr; opt_clean" driver_constant.v` gives no warnings.

It seems wrong for something that has the possibility to cause significant unexpected behaviour (and arguably should be an error), to not even produce a warning depending on the sequence of passes called.
